hero_episodes.headline: Boardy, YC cloning pressure, and the opinions-vs-data founder test

trajectory_delta.now_label: $8M seed reported Jan 14, 2025 via TechCrunch, led by Creandum
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: Narrative shifted from AI novelty to capital-backed network product.

trajectory_delta.then_label: Publicly framing inference cost as the core enterprise AI bottleneck
trajectory_delta.now_label: Neurometric positioned as inference optimization / routing platform; Rob also MD at HalfCourt Ventures
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: Moat thesis is moving from models to the system-of-context layer.

trajectory_delta.now_label: v2 described publicly as adding source-of-truth layer, orchestration, and meeting-note ingestion
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: The market is rewarding operational wrappers, not generic chat fronts.

trajectory_delta.then_label: AI video founder in category-building phase
trajectory_delta.now_label: Synthesia raised a $180M round at a $2.1B valuation in Jan 2025 with NEA backing and existing investors including GV
trajectory_delta.intervention_note: Proof that enterprise AI media can become a durable software category.

the_misses.at_the_time: Regulatory risk and belief that black-car demand was niche
the_misses.logic_then: The service looked boxed in by taxi regulation and a seemingly small premium transport market.
the_misses.reality_now: Uber expanded the market by making point-to-point transport dramatically easier, faster, and more available than taxis.
the_misses.lesson: Do not size disruptive markets by the old category's footprint.

the_misses.at_the_time: Trust, safety, and social discomfort looked fatal
the_misses.logic_then: People would not reliably sleep in strangers' homes, so mainstream adoption looked impossible.
the_misses.reality_now: Ratings, identity, and review loops created a software trust layer that unlocked global supply and demand.
the_misses.lesson: When software removes a deep human trust barrier, category size can reset upward fast.

masterclass_featured.title: Anthropic’s Mythos is a cyber-weapon, so you can’t have it

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Opinions vs. Data
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: Strong opinions are useful only if they cash out in measured outcomes.

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Inference Cost Rule
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: If your AI product cannot compress inference cost, it is renting growth not owning it.

masterclass_sidebar_formulas.label: Agent Value Test
masterclass_sidebar_formulas.formula: An AI agent matters only when it removes actual headcount or workflow load.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: Mythos as strategic threat surface
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: The episode frames frontier model access as a geopolitical and cyber risk, not a normal SaaS feature launch.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: OpenClaw subsidy era is over
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: E2272 centers the transition from subsidized model access to explicit operating economics for agent builders.

masterclass_sidebar_insights.title: Boardy and the Barksdale filter
masterclass_sidebar_insights.body: E2271 sharpens the founder test: narrative is cheap, data-backed product behavior is what compounds.
